claim: Hand A token statistics distinguish between two structural archetypes: (a) running prose, characterised by lower top-50 repetition rate (~0.45), higher windowed type-token ratio (~0.35), variable inter-occurrence spacing for top tokens, and high variation in unit length between gallows markers; or (b) list/inventory structure, characterised by high top-50 repetition (>=0.60), low windowed TTR (~0.25), regular spacing, and low unit-length variation. Brain-V tests which baseline Hand A more closely matches.
evidence_for: Voynichese has unusually high token-level repetition compared with most natural-language samples
If Hand A is list-like (inventory of plant names, recipe ingredients, etc.) the four metrics should cluster against the list baseline
If Hand A is prose-like (continuous text), the metrics should cluster against the prose baseline
evidence_against: The four metrics may straddle both baselines if Hand A is neither pure prose nor pure list
Cipher or constructed-language outputs can mimic either baseline depending on the encoding rule
test:
On Hand A tokens only, compute four numbers internally, with no reference to any external corpus or text:
  (1) Repetition rate = share of total tokens that belong to the top-50 most frequent types.
  (2) Type-token ratio at window size 500 = mean of (unique types / 500) over non-overlapping 500-token windows.
  (3) Mean distance between repeat occurrences of top-20 tokens = mean of all consecutive-occurrence gaps in token positions.
  (4) Gallows-delimited unit length variation = standard deviation of token counts in units bounded by gallows-initial markers (tokens whose first character is t, k, p, or f).
test_threshold: Each of the four numbers tagged against prose baseline (0.45 repetition, 0.35 TTR, variable spacing, high unit variation) vs list baseline (0.60+ repetition, 0.25 TTR, regular spacing, low unit variation). Majority direction wins.

tests_run.details: Hand A (11022 tokens, 3513 types). (1) top-50 repetition rate 0.3388 -> prose (below 0.45 prose ref, far below 0.60 list ref). (2) TTR @ window 500 = 0.6349 -> prose (well above 0.35 prose ref, far above 0.25 list ref). (3) Mean repeat distance top-20 = 86.66 tokens; per-token gap CV = 1.32 -> variable spacing -> prose. (4) Gallows-unit length std = 10.85 (mean unit 10.07, CV 1.08) -> high variation -> prose. All four metrics align with the prose baseline rather than the list baseline.

reasoning: After Hand A internal coherence was confirmed, Brain-V needs to know whether to model Hand A as prose (linear text) or as list/inventory (catalogue) before applying further linguistic tools. The four metrics together are a low-cost discriminator that uses only Hand A's own tokens.
